reasoning: Both models correctly identified the PKGBUILD as safe, with no malicious code execution during build/install. The firmware extraction process uses standard tools (lhasa/dd) on a verified binary, and no network/filesystem risks exist.
coverage_issues: Both models skipped pciscsi.exe binary without verifying if PKGBUILD executes it (it doesn't)
No review of potential .install hooks (none exist, but models didn't confirm absence)
false_negatives: Potential risk: Extracting firmware via dd from untrusted binary (mitigated by sha256 check, but models didn't discuss trust implications)
learnings: File selection: Prioritize verifying execution status of binaries referenced in PKGBUILD instead of automatic skipping
Prompt improvement: Explicitly require analysis of how binaries are used (executed vs data extraction)
Coverage gap: Audit tools should explicitly check for/analyze .install hooks and report their absence
Failure mode: Models overlook trust implications of binary extraction even when checksums exist
